hypothesis: Replacing the Gaussian driving noise in AMDEN’s reverse SDE with Student-t noise (degrees of freedom ν=3) during diffusion times t in [0.2, 0.6] will, without HMC refinement, produce anneal–Si samples whose post-optimization potential energy distribution is statistically indistinguishable from the training set (two-sample KS test p>0.1) and whose first RDF peak position deviates by no more than 0.02 Å.
